---
title: "HPC End-to-End Testing"
subtitle: "Running the pipeline on Rockfish (SLURM/Singularity)"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## Run tracking template

Copy `templates/hpc_integration_test.qmd` into your working notes to track each
test run. It provides all the commands below in execution order with output
placeholders and a completion checklist.
:::

The `rockfish` profile targets the Johns Hopkins Rockfish cluster. These steps
cover environment setup, running the pipeline end-to-end, collecting integration
test data, and syncing results back to your local machine for test execution.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef step fill:#9b59b6,stroke:#6c3483,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    ENV["Activate nf24_env conda env"]:::step
    VCF["Generate test VCF (for local test profiles)"]:::step
    STRAIN["generate_hpc_strainfile.sh (for HPC profiles)"]:::step
    RUN["nextflow run -profile test_hpc,rockfish --legacy_assess"]:::step
    WAIT(["SLURM jobs complete"]):::data
    COLLECT["collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc --collect-only"]:::step
    RSYNC["rsync integration_data/ to local machine"]:::step
    TEST["Rscript tests/run_tests.R with TEST_DB_DIR set"]:::step
    DONE(["All tests pass"]):::data

    ENV --> VCF
    ENV --> STRAIN --> RUN --> WAIT --> COLLECT --> RSYNC --> TEST --> DONE
```


## Environment Setup

```bash
conda activate /data/eande106/software/conda_envs/nf24_env
export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR=/vast/eande106/singularity
```


## Prerequisites

- Nextflow `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0` (provided by the `nf24_env` conda environment)
- SLURM account `eande106`; partition `parallel` (default) and `bigmem` (legacy
  QTL detection only)
- Singularity images cached at `/vast/eande106/singularity` (configured via
  `singularity.cacheDir` in `conf/rockfish.config`)


## Running the Pipeline on Rockfish

```bash
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv
  # vcf is specified per-group in the strainfile (see README: Strainfile Format)
```

The `rockfish` profile sets `executor = 'slurm'`, enables Singularity with
`autoMounts = true`, and applies per-process CPU/RAM/time/retry settings. Key
process resources from `conf/rockfish.config`:

| Process group | CPUs | Memory | Time | Max retries |
|--------------|------|--------|------|-------------|
| bcftools/plink extraction | 4 | 40 GB | 1 h | 3 |
| Eigen computation (`r_find_genotype_matrix_eigen`) | 2 | 60 GB | 30 min | 3 |
| GWA (simulate, GRM, map) | 4 | 20 GB | 10 min | 3 |
| DB migration (write marker set) | 2 | 8 GB | 30 min | 3 |
| DB migration (other) | 1 | 2–8 GB | 5–20 min | 2–3 |
| `r_get_gcta_intervals` (bigmem) | 1 | — | 4 h × attempt | 5 |


## Generating Test VCFs on Rockfish

All test VCF generation scripts must run inside the bcftools Singularity container. `--bind /vast/eande106` makes the source VCF paths
accessible inside the container; `--bind "$PWD"` ensures the project directory is
writable so generated files land on the host filesystem.

```bash
SIMG=/vast/eande106/singularity/quay.io-biocontainers-bcftools-1.16--hfe4b78e_1.img
```

### Single-species profiles (`test`, `test_variable`, `test_cv_pool`)

Only the C. elegans VCF is needed. `generate_test_vcf.sh` subsets the full CE VCF to
200 strains and chromosomes I, II, V and rewrites `data/test/test_strains.txt`:

```bash
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

Output: `data/test/test_ce.vcf.gz` (gitignored) and dated symlink
`test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz`. See `data/test/release_ids.sh` for the currently pinned
CE release date.

### Three-species profile (`test_three_species`)

The `test_three_species` profile requires a separate test VCF for each species.
Use the per-species scripts — they apply random variant sampling (15% of variants, for
manageable local PLINK marker counts) and update `data/test/test_strains_three_species.txt`:

```bash
# C. elegans
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_ce_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# C. briggsae
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_cb_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_briggsae/WI/variation/20250626/vcf/WI.20250626.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# C. tropicalis
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_ct_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_tropicalis/WI/variation/20250627/vcf/WI.20250627.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

See `data/test/release_ids.sh` for the currently pinned release dates.


## Generating HPC Strainfiles

HPC profiles (`test_hpc`, `test_hpc_variable`, `test_hpc_cv_pool`, `test_hpc_three_species`)
use strainfiles that embed absolute Rockfish VCF paths, so the pipeline reads VCFs directly
from the `/vast/eande106` filesystem rather than downloading from CaeNDR S3.

`data/test/hpc_strains.txt` and `data/test/hpc_strains_three_species.txt` are committed to
git as header-only placeholders. Run `generate_hpc_strainfile.sh` on Rockfish to populate them, using the same
Singularity container as above:

```bash
SIMG=/vast/eande106/singularity/quay.io-biocontainers-bcftools-1.16--hfe4b78e_1.img

# CE only — updates hpc_strains.txt (used by test_hpc, test_hpc_variable, test_hpc_cv_pool):
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# All three species — updates both strainfiles (also needed for test_hpc_three_species):
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_briggsae/WI/variation/20250626/vcf/WI.20250626.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_tropicalis/WI/variation/20250627/vcf/WI.20250627.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

See `data/test/release_ids.sh` for the currently pinned CaeNDR release dates.


## Profile Reference

All four local test profiles run on Rockfish (SLURM) by combining with `rockfish`. The four
HPC profiles (`test_hpc*`) also run on Rockfish but use full CaeNDR VCFs from `/vast/eande106`
instead of the 200-strain test subset:

| Profile combination | VCF source | Strainfile | Legacy assess |
|--------------------|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| `-profile test,rockfish` | test VCF (200 strains) | `test_strains.txt` | ✓ |
| `-profile test_variable,rockfish` | test VCF (200 strains) | `test_strains.txt` | ✓ |
| `-profile test_three_species,rockfish` | test VCF (200 strains) | `test_strains_three_species.txt` | ✓ |
| `-profile test_cv_pool,rockfish` | test VCF (200 strains) | `test_strains.txt` | ✗ |
| `-profile test_hpc,rockfish` | full VCF (all strains) | `hpc_strains.txt` | ✓ |
| `-profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish` | full VCF (all strains) | `hpc_strains.txt` | ✓ |
| `-profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish` | full VCF (all strains) | `hpc_strains_three_species.txt` | ✓ |
| `-profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish` | full VCF (all strains) | `hpc_strains.txt` | ✗ |

HPC strainfiles must be populated with `generate_hpc_strainfile.sh` before using `test_hpc*`
profiles (see [Generating HPC Strainfiles](#generating-hpc-strainfiles) above).


## Collecting Integration Test Data on Rockfish

Run the pipeline on the cluster for each profile, then use `--collect-only` on the login node
to gather outputs without re-running. Each profile stores outputs in
`tests/integration_data/<profile>/` so multiple profiles can be collected independently.

**Prerequisite:** Generate the test VCFs before running any local test profile on Rockfish
(see [Generating Test VCFs on Rockfish](#generating-test-vcfs-on-rockfish)).

### Run commands

Each profile uses `--output_dir` to write to a profile-named directory. This prevents
same-day runs from overwriting each other and ensures `collect_test_data.sh --collect-only`
picks up the correct output for each profile.

```bash
# Fixed architecture (equivalent to local `test` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test

# Variable architecture grid (equivalent to local `test_variable` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_variable,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_variable \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-variable

# Three-species — requires all three test VCFs (see Generating Test VCFs above)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_three_species,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_three_species \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-three-species

# CV pool — omit --legacy_assess (incompatible with non-marker causal variants)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_cv_pool,rockfish \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_cv_pool \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-cv-pool
```

- `--output_dir` gives each profile a stable, named output directory. Without it, the default
  `Analysis_Results-{date}` collides when multiple profiles are run on the same day.
- `-work-dir` redirects intermediate files to `/scratch4/` (high-capacity scratch); without
  it Nextflow defaults to `./work/` on the login node's local disk, which is too small for
  a full pipeline run.
- Replace `<yourname>` with your Rockfish username (e.g. `Ryan`).
- `--legacy_assess` is required to generate the legacy assessment file used by the
  cross-validation tests. Omit it for `test_cv_pool`.

### Collecting outputs

After each run completes, collect outputs on the login node:

```bash
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test --collect-only
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable --collect-only
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_three_species --collect-only
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_cv_pool --collect-only
```

**Sync data to your local machine** using `rsync` from your local terminal (not
from Rockfish). `<rockfish-host>` is whatever SSH alias or hostname you use
to connect — check `~/.ssh/config` for a `Host` entry. If you have an alias
like `Host rockfish`, use `rockfish`; otherwise use the full form
`<yourname>@login.rockfish.jhu.edu`.

```bash
# Run from your local machine — syncs db/, assessment TSVs, and cross-validation
# files (work/) for all collected profiles in a single command
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:/vast/eande106/<yourname>/nemascan-sims-nf/tests/integration_data/ \
  tests/integration_data/
```

The `--delete` flag keeps the local directory in exact sync with Rockfish
(removes any local files that no longer exist on the cluster). The trailing
`/` on both paths syncs the *contents* of the directory rather than the
directory itself.

### Running integration tests locally

#### `test` profile (fixed architecture, 1 population, 4 mappings)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

#### `test_variable` profile (variable architecture, 1 population)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/work \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=256 \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

#### `test_three_species` profile (3 species, 9 populations, 36 mappings)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/work \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS="ce.test.200strains,ce.test.200strains.popA,ce.test.200strains.popB,cb.test.200strains,cb.test.200strains.popA,cb.test.200strains.popB,ct.test.200strains,ct.test.200strains.popA,ct.test.200strains.popB" \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

#### `test_cv_pool` profile (cv pool, no legacy assessment)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/work \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Environment variables

- `TEST_WORK_DIR` points to a directory containing cross-validation files (`.fastGWA`,
  `.mlma`, `*_total_independent_tests.txt`). The `collect_test_data.sh` script extracts
  only these files from the full Nextflow work directory into a per-profile `work/`
  subdirectory. If unset, `test-cross_validation.R` skips automatically.
- `TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS` defaults to 4. Override for multi-parameter profiles.
- `TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS` defaults to 1. Override for multi-population profiles.
- `TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS` is required for multi-population assertions (three-species).
  When unset, multi-population tests skip.
:::


## Stub Runs on Rockfish

Stub runs bypass SLURM submission and verify process wiring without executing
real computations:

```bash
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv \
  -stub-run
  # vcf is specified per-group in the strainfile (see README: Strainfile Format)
```

## Running HPC Integration Test Profiles

Four HPC profiles mirror the local test profiles but use full CaeNDR VCFs on the Rockfish
filesystem. Each combines with `rockfish` and optionally `--legacy_assess`. Use `--output_dir`
to give each profile a named output directory — this prevents same-day runs from overwriting
each other and ensures `collect_test_data.sh --collect-only` finds the correct output.

```bash
# Fixed architecture (equivalent to local `test` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc

# Variable architecture grid (equivalent to local `test_variable` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_variable \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable

# Three-species (requires hpc_strains_three_species.txt to be populated)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_three_species \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species

# CV pool — omit --legacy_assess (incompatible with non-marker causal variants)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_cv_pool \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
```

### Collecting HPC outputs

After each run completes, collect outputs on the login node. Pass `--work-dir` to also
collect the cross-validation files (`.fastGWA`, `.mlma`, EIGEN files) — only these specific
files are extracted from the work directory, not the full contents (which can be 100+ GB).

```bash
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_variable --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_three_species --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_cv_pool --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
```

The collect script looks for `Analysis_Results-<profile>/` first, then falls back to
the most recent `Analysis_Results-*` directory. Cross-validation files are stored in
`tests/integration_data/<profile>/work/` alongside `db/` and assessment TSVs.

### Syncing to local machine

Run from your local terminal (not Rockfish). A single rsync syncs everything — db/,
assessment TSVs, and cross-validation work files — for all collected profiles:

```bash
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:/vast/eande106/<yourname>/nemascan-sims-nf/tests/integration_data/ \
  tests/integration_data/
```

### Running HPC integration tests locally

HPC profiles use population name `ce.hpc` (not `ce.test.200strains`), so `TEST_POPULATION`
must be set for all HPC test invocations.

#### `test_hpc` profile (fixed architecture, 1 population, 4 mappings)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

#### `test_hpc_variable` profile (variable architecture, 1 population, 260 mappings)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=260 \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

#### `test_hpc_three_species` profile (9 populations, 36 mappings)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS="ce.hpc,ce.hpc.popA,ce.hpc.popB,cb.hpc,cb.hpc.popA,cb.hpc.popB,ct.hpc,ct.hpc.popA,ct.hpc.popB" \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

If the three-species profile was run with `--legacy_assess`, also add:

```
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
```

#### `test_hpc_cv_pool` profile (cv pool, no legacy assessment)

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## After collecting HPC data
Run the full integration test suite locally:
[Local Integration Testing](testing-integration.qmd)
:::


## Cleaning Up Work Directories

After collecting integration test data and syncing to your local machine, remove the
Nextflow work directories from scratch to free space on `/scratch4/`.

Run these commands **on Rockfish** (not your local machine). Replace `<yourname>` with
your Rockfish username.

### Local test profiles

```bash
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-variable
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-three-species
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-cv-pool
```

### HPC profiles

```bash
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## Verify data is synced before deleting

Confirm your local `tests/integration_data/` is up to date and integration tests pass
before removing work directories. The cross-validation test (`test-cross_validation.R`)
reads raw GWA files from the work directory — once deleted, that test will skip
automatically.
:::
